summary: Photographer app focused strictly on sports and concert photography to showcase a specialized portfolio and manage client session bookings with approval workflow and optional payments.
architectural_notes: Scope/brand direction: app is strictly for sports and concert photography (portfolio categories, copy, and theme should reflect this niche).

existing_features.title: Create a public-facing portfolio section where visitors can browse the photographer’s work, including a homepage/landing page, a gallery grid, and individual project/photo detail views.

Implement backend data models and APIs to store and retrieve portfolio items (including images as frontend static asset references/URLs), and support photographer-only create/update/delete of portfolio items.
Create a client session booking flow that lets visitors request a session by submitting contact info, preferred date/time, session type, location/notes, and optional reference links.
Add a photographer dashboard (Internet Identity login) to review booking requests and accept or deny them, including the ability to propose/confirm a scheduled time and add internal notes.

Add basic payment tracking for sessions, including setting a price/quote for a booking, recording payment status (Unpaid/Paid/Refunded), and allowing the photographer to mark a booking as paid (manual/offline) from the dashboard.

Add a coherent, distinctive visual theme for a professional photography brand and apply it consistently across the landing page, gallery, booking flow, and dashboard (colors, typography, spacing, and components).
Add generated static brand imagery and integrate it into the UI (served from frontend/public/assets/generated), including a logo and a homepage hero image.

Update all public-facing UI copy and labels so the app is explicitly and consistently positioned as a sports and concert photography portfolio and booking service (remove references to weddings, portraits, family, corporate, and generic event photography).

Constrain portfolio and booking inputs in the UI to sports and concert photography only by updating form controls and placeholders to use sports/concert-specific options instead of freeform/general-purpose lists.
Add a public contact information area to the site so visitors can find the photographer’s contact details and an Instagram profile link.
Make the contact details (email text and Instagram URL/handle) easy to update by centralizing them in a single frontend constant/config location and rendering from that source in the contact area.

new_feature_requests.summary: Portfolio showcase section for photographer's work (gallery/featured projects)
Client session booking system (request and schedule sessions)
Photographer workflow to accept or deny booking requests
Payments support for sessions (collect payments during/after booking)
Implement role/ownership rules so only the photographer account can manage portfolio items and booking approvals, while public users can only submit booking requests and view the public portfolio.
Update branding, content categories, and UI copy to be strictly sports and concert photography (portfolio/booking context)

Enforce sports and concert-only constraints in the backend by validating portfolio item category and booking session type values on create/update, rejecting any non-sports/concert values with clear English error messages.
Add a contact information section including an Instagram profile link (e.g., in footer/contact page) for visitors to reach the photographer.

projectName: Photographer portfolio + session booking with approval and payment tracking
